cut off mark: The general cut-off is 170. However, Law and Community Health require 200. Engineering, Computer Science, and Nutrition require 180.
how to apply: Visit ug.nsuk.edu.ng/application, create a profile, select '2025/2026 Post-UTME', and pay the ₦2,000 fee.
direct entry: DE applicants need a Lower Credit in ND/NSUK Diploma, Merit in NCE, or 8 points in IJMB, plus 5 O'Level credits.
requirements: You need at least 5 O'Level credits (including English and Maths) in no more than 2 sittings and must upload them to JAMB CAPS.
jamb caps: You MUST upload your O'Level results at an accredited CBT center. If they aren't on CAPS, NSUK cannot offer you admission.
accept admission: Once offered admission on JAMB CAPS, you have 4 weeks to accept it, or the offer will be permanently cancelled.
post utme date: For the 2025/2026 session, registration ran from July 28 to September 12. Monitor the portal for 2026/2027 dates.
phone number: Always use your personal phone number during registration; it is linked to your profile for all future university communications.
french: Good news! Mathematics and French O'Level requirements are waived for candidates applying specifically for the French program.
health science: DE candidates for Community Health or Environmental Health must possess a valid license from their professional bodies.
